title: The Sleeping Tide
body: The sea has left three treasures for brave explorers. Find them before the tide wakes and the next wave-song begins.

title: Shell Listeners
body: The merrow left five singing shells along the waterline last night. Gather them and line them up from smallest voice to loudest.

title: Gull Dancers
body: The silver gulls are teaching their storm-dance to anyone brave enough to learn. Watch their wings closely, then show me the pattern.

title: Captain Greysail's Landing
body: A ghost ship arrived at midnight and left footprints in the sand. Follow Captain Greysail's trail and tell me what treasure he buried.

title: Wave Jumpers
body: The wave-riders are testing you with ten rolling waves. Jump over every third one and bow low beneath the rest.

title: Foam Collectors
body: The sea is weaving cloud-bubbles where the waves break white. Catch three handfuls of foam before they melt back into salt water.

title: Crab Messengers
body: The sideways-walking crabs carry urgent messages from rock pool to rock pool. Move like them—low, swift, and never in a straight line.

title: The Serpent's Resting Place
body: A sea serpent needs somewhere safe to sleep before sunset. Build it a castle of sand, strong enough to hold dreams.

title: Tide Pool Watchers
body: A tiny kingdom lives in this pool between the rocks. Kneel down like a giant and count every creature that moves.

title: The Uncharted Isle
body: Use driftwood and stones to map an island no sailor has ever found. Mark where the dragon sleeps and where the treasure's buried.

title: The Moss Path
body: The greenway only appears for those who step softly. Walk only on moss, never bare earth, until you find something perfectly round.

title: Leaf Weavers
body: The forest keeper needs seven different leaf shapes for an ancient pattern. Find them and lay them in a circle beneath the oldest tree.

title: Bridge for the Small Folk
body: The beetle kingdom can't cross the muddy stream without help. Build them a bridge from fallen twigs and bark.

title: Tree Whisperers
body: Find the oldest tree—the one the birds call Elder. Press your ear against its bark and listen for the heartbeat underneath.

title: Root Walkers
body: The twisting roots are an enchanted balance course set by the wood sprites. Walk heel-to-toe along them without touching the ground.

title: The Hidden Door
body: Somewhere here stands a door to the hollow hills, hidden where only sharp eyes look. Search low—between roots, under ferns, behind peeling bark.

title: The Forest's Song
body: The thrushes are waiting for you to join the morning chorus. Sing three clear notes, then listen and copy whatever the wood sings back.

title: Tracks of the Stag King
body: Something with silver antlers passed through at dawn and left signs. Follow the clues—hoof prints, bent grass, scattered acorns—and tell me where it went.

title: The Wishing Circle
body: Build a ring of stones, acorns, and feathers in a place where sunlight touches the ground. Stand inside, close your eyes, and speak one true wish.

title: Fern Counters
body: The fiddlehead ferns are uncurling like questions the forest is asking. Find a patch and count how many are still curled tight in their dreams.

title: Shadow Thieves
body: Your shadow is trying to slip away across the grass and hide. Chase it, catch it, then see if you can make it vanish completely.

title: Pigeon Messengers
body: The grey messengers are delivering invisible letters to every tree in the park. Count them as they fly and guess what news they're carrying.

title: Grass Sigils
body: Flatten a secret pattern in the long grass—a spell that only works when seen from above. Make it big enough for passing clouds to read.

title: The Memory Bench
body: This bench remembers everyone who's ever sat here. Sit down, close your eyes, and imagine the person who was here yesterday. What were they dreaming about?

title: Tightrope Walkers
body: The path's edge is an invisible rope stretched high above a canyon. Walk it like an acrobat, arms wide, until you reach the safety of the tree.

title: Cloud Namers
body: The sky-shepherds need help naming today's clouds. Lie on your back, find three different ones, give them proper names, and wave as they journey on.

title: The Frame Builders
body: Collect four sticks blessed by the wind—one straight, one crooked, one short, one long. Fit them together to frame an invisible painting.

title: Wind Listeners
body: The wind is playing an old tune in the treetops, older than any song you know. Stand perfectly still and hum along until you learn it.

title: The Silly Sprint
body: We'll race to that far tree, but there's a rule: we must both run in the silliest way the park has ever seen. Choose your style now.

title: Petal Gatherers
body: The flowers have scattered their coloured blessings on the ground like confetti. Gather a handful and arrange them in a rainbow for the insects to admire.
